"'''A Death Star Is Born'''" is a 12-page comic featured in [[Star Wars Tales 4|''Star Wars Tales'' 4]], written by [[Kevin Rubio]]. It was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 1]]''.

==Plot summary==
[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], [[Tol Sivron]], and [[Bevel Lemelisk]] present their [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] proposal to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. The perceived flaws of the schematics are humorously discussed, such as that the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] are easy to shut off, owing to the low intellects of the common [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], and there are no support beams on the bridges because it would be too expensive to add them. The Emperor also notes the exhaust port as being too large at around fifteen meters wide, prompting them to reduce its size to the [[canon]]ical two meters—the smallest possible safe size—and add multiple [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] to the trench to decrease the chance that a pilot could make the necessary shot.

==Development==
The title of the sketch is similar to the [[1930s|1937]] movie [[Wikipedia:A Star Is Born (1937 film)|''A Star is Born'']], which has been remade three times, in [[Wikipedia:A Star Is Born (1954 film)|1954]], [[Wikipedia:A Star Is Born (1976 film)|1976]], and (sixteen years after the comic was published) [[Wikipedia:A Star Is Born (2018 film)|2018]].

==Continuity==
The appearance of Bevel Lemelisk is different in this comic than in any other source where he is portrayed. This was an intentional choice on behalf of the comic's creator, although for unknown reasons. One should also note the presence of a [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|particular Mon Calamari]].

When the [[Maw Installation]] team approach the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on their [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], also visible are the [https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/USS_Enterprise_(NCC-1701) original Starship ''Enterprise''], a [[Wikipedia:Big Boy (restaurant)|Bob's Big Boy]], [[Wikipedia:Big Guy and Rusty|BGY-11 "Big Guy" robot]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] (well, a [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300]] at any rate), and the [https://beatles.fandom.com/wiki/Yellow_Submarine_(film) "Yellow Submarine"].

The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] emblems in the Palace are in the shape of [https://disney.fandom.com/wiki/Mickey_Mouse Mickey Mouse ears]. Also, when Darth Vader gives Tarkin [[coffee]], he offers him a milk carton with a cow pictured on it. The hologram Lemelisk accidentally presents, claiming it belongs to his daughter, features [https://yogibear.fandom.com/wiki/Yogi_Bear_(cartoon_character) Yogi Bear].

Names offered for the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] include "[[Wikipedia:Planet killer|Planet Killer]]" (a possible reference to [https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/The_Doomsday_Machine_(episode) ''The Doomsday Machine'']), "Death From Above" (a possible reference to [[Wikipedia:Apocalypse Now|''Apocalypse Now'']] or [https://rocky.fandom.com/wiki/Ivan_Drago Ivan Drago's] nickname in [https://rocky.fandom.com/wiki/Rocky_IV ''Rocky IV'']), and [[Wikipedia:RMS Titanic|''Titanic'']].
